notes: Rounds is a polyphonic synthesizer running inside Reaktor 6 with a unique architecture: eight independent voices, each capable of its own distinct synthesis method including analog-style subtractive, FM, and waveshaping. This voice-per-synthesis approach creates complex, evolving textures that sound different from traditional single-engine polysynths. The primary expressive controls are Filter Cutoff and the Voice Spread/Detune which controls how widely the eight voices are detuned from each other — this is the sonic centrepiece. CC 74 (Brightness) maps to the filter cutoff. CC 1 (Modulation) controls LFO depth or effects blend depending on the preset, enabling mod wheel-based textural evolution. Rounds is particularly well-suited to evolving pads, atmospheric textures, and complex harmonic soundscapes. Quick Control layout below reflects typical default assignments and may vary per preset in Reaktor.

Volume.description: Master output level of the Reaktor ensemble

Expression.description: Dynamic expression / output level — shapes the level of the complex polyphonic texture

Modulation.description: LFO Depth or Effects Blend — mod wheel modulates texture and motion in real time

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds notes; useful for building layered textural chords

Release Time.description: Envelope release time — controls the length of tails on each of the eight voices

Attack Time.description: Envelope attack time — from instant to slow swells across the voice bank

Brightness.description: Filter Cutoff — primary tonal control across the primary synthesis engine

Reverb Send.description: Reverb wet level — adds spatial depth to the complex multi-voice texture

Quick Control 1.description: Primary Synth Filter Cutoff — the main filter cutoff for the primary synthesis engine across all voices

Quick Control 2.description: Resonance — filter resonance amount; adds character and harmonic emphasis

Quick Control 3.description: Attack — amplitude and filter envelope attack time across all voices

Quick Control 4.description: Release — amplitude and filter envelope release; longer for ambient, shorter for rhythmic patches

Quick Control 5.description: LFO Rate — speed of the low-frequency oscillator modulation applied to filter or pitch

Quick Control 6.description: LFO Depth / Mix — intensity of LFO modulation; blends in modulation character

Quick Control 7.description: Effects Blend — overall wet/dry mix of the internal effects chain (chorus, reverb, delay)

Quick Control 8.description: Voice Spread / Detune — amount of detuning and spectral spreading between the eight independent voices; core tonal parameter
